_comment: Statusline config. Edit templates, weather location and colors here without touching statusline.py. This file MUST live in the same folder as statusline.py. Colors live in the 'colors' block below (hex #RRGGBB), referenced from templates as {c.NAME}; any color key omitted there keeps the COLORS default in statusline.py. If this file is missing/invalid, statusline.py falls back to its built-in defaults. Keys starting with _ are ignored.
_comment_emoji_width: How many terminal columns one emoji occupies (1 or 2). Used only to align the line2 context bar under the line1 segment. Most modern terminals (Windows Terminal, iTerm2) render emoji 2-wide; set 1 if your terminal renders them single-width and the bar looks shifted. Anything other than 1 means 2.
_comment_ctx_bar_empty: Glyph used for the context bar's UNFILLED cells (the ░ track). Any single-column character works, e.g. ░ ▒ ▓ · - or a single space. Colored with the ctx_bar_track color. Should be 1 column wide to keep the bar alignment correct.

weather._comment: City name + coordinates for the Open-Meteo API (fetched only when an active line uses {weather} or {sun}). show_* booleans pick which parts of the {weather} string appear (all default true). The editor's 'Look up coordinates' action geocodes the name for you.
weather.name: Newyork

templates._comment: Available placeholders: {c.COLORNAME} apply color, {r} reset, {time} {version} {model} {effort} {ctx} {ctx_percent} {ctx_bar} {align_pad} {total} {input} {output} {cached} {path} {branch} {added} {removed} {weather} {sun} {peak_label}. Bare {ctx_bar} auto-sizes/positions to align line2's gauge with the line1 segment from {effort} to end-of-line; put {align_pad} right before it for the leading spaces. Use {ctx_bar:N} for a FIXED N-cell bar (e.g. {ctx_bar:30}, no {align_pad} needed). Eighth-block precision either way. Lines are printed in order; remove a line to hide it.

colors._comment: Color overrides (hex #RRGGBB), used in templates as {c.KEY}. Delete a key to fall back to the statusline.py default. Invalid values are ignored. ctx_bar = filled gauge blocks, ctx_bar_track = empty ░ cells, ctx_bracket = the ▐ ▌ edges.
